figures.caption: Figure 1: The pipelines of Traditional Agent Engineering and the proposed TPGO in multi-agent system optimization.

Table 1: Performance on the MCP-Universe benchmark, an exploratory optimization scenario. TPGO significantly enhances the success rate of ReAct agents across all four complex task domains by learning from execution feedback.
Table 7: Design of the Corrective Modification
Table 8: Content of the Resulting Prompt Modification
Table 6: Analysis of a Representative Trajectory Failure

Figure 2: Overview of the Textual Parameter Graph Optimization (TPGO) framework. The framework operates in a closed-loop cycle: (1) Graph Construction, where unstructured agent prompts are disentangled into a structured Textual Parameter Graph (TPG); (2) Gradient-Driven Evolution, which generates semantic Textual Gradients from execution trajectories to diagnose faults; and (3) Group Relative Agent Optimization (GRAO), a meta-learning module that leverages an optimization experience memory to guide the Optimizer LLM in generating effective graph updates (∆G).
